---
title: 'system.fail_points'
slug: /operations/system-tables/fail_points
description: 'Contains a list of all available failpoints with their type.'

---

## Description {#description}

Contains a list of all available failpoints registered in the server, along with their type.

Failpoints can be enabled and disabled at runtime using the `SYSTEM ENABLE FAILPOINT` and `SYSTEM DISABLE FAILPOINT` statements.

## Columns {#columns}

<!--AUTOGENERATED_START-->
- `name` ([String](/sql-reference/data-types/string)) — Name of the failpoint.
- `type` ([Enum8](/sql-reference/data-types/enum)) — Type of the failpoint. Possible values:
  - `'once'` — Triggers a single time and then auto-disables.
  - `'regular'` — Triggers every time the failpoint is hit.
  - `'pauseable_once'` — Blocks execution once until explicitly resumed.
  - `'pauseable'` — Blocks execution every time the failpoint is hit until explicitly resumed.
<!--AUTOGENERATED_END-->

## Example {#example}

```sql title="Query"
SYSTEM ENABLE FAILPOINT replicated_merge_tree_insert_retry_pause;
SELECT * FROM system.fail_points WHERE name = 'replicated_merge_tree_insert_retry_pause'
```

```text title="Response"
┌─name──────────────────────────────────────┬─type────────────┐
│ replicated_merge_tree_insert_retry_pause  │ pauseable_once  │
└───────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘
```
